immutable-by-default.evidence: Cart and CartItem interfaces use `readonly` on all properties (lines 41-48). ShippingResult also readonly (lines 51-56). `calculateShipping` returns a new object literal (lines 116-122), never mutates the input cart.

no-magic-numbers.evidence: Every numeric literal is a named constant: `HEAVY_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD_KG = 50`, `MEDIUM_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD_KG = 20`, `MAX_WEIGHT_KG = 500`, `HEAVY_RATE_PER_KG = 0.5`, `HEAVY_SURCHARGE = 15`, `MEDIUM_RATE_PER_KG = 0.3`, `MEDIUM_SURCHARGE = 8`, `LIGHT_FLAT_COST = 5` (lines 11-19). Country multipliers in `COUNTRY_SHIPPING_MULTIPLIER` record (lines 23-30).

strict-typing.evidence: No `any` anywhere. All functions have explicit parameter and return types. Uses `asserts country is SupportedCountry` type predicate (line 66). Domain types defined as interfaces with readonly fields.

externalize-config.evidence: All rates and thresholds are top-level named constants (lines 9-36): weight thresholds, per-kg rates, surcharges, country multipliers, and valid coupon codes.

bound-every-input.evidence: `assertValidWeight` throws on non-finite, <= 0, or > MAX_WEIGHT_KG (lines 60-63). `assertSupportedCountry` throws on unknown country (lines 66-70). `assertValidCoupon` throws on invalid coupon (lines 73-76). Empty cart throws (line 100). No silent fallbacks or defaults anywhere.
